title: Crunchy Chocolatey Vietnamese Shrimp Cha Gio
description: A malty and springy Vietnamese dish featuring dark chocolate chips, perfect for a hangover cure, made with brine-wet and low-sodium-friendly ingredients in the form of cha gio.
instructions: Soak the rice paper wrappers in warm water for 30 seconds to soften them.
In a large bowl, combine the shrimp, dark chocolate chips, mung bean noodles, carrots, bean sprouts, and green onions.
Add the fish sauce and lime juice to the bowl and mix well.
Lay a softened rice paper wrapper on a flat surface and place about 1/4 cup of the shrimp mixture in the center of the wrapper.
Fold the bottom half of the wrapper up over the filling, then fold in the sides and roll up the wrapper to form a tight cylinder.
Repeat with the remaining wrappers and filling.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
When the oil is hot, add a few of the cha gio to the skillet and fry until they are golden brown and crispy, about 3-4 minutes on each side.
Remove the cha gio from the oil and drain on paper towels.
Serve the cha gio hot with your favorite dipping sauce.
